status_reason: This is the frozen Phase 1 candidate/source snapshot. All five candidates have explicit legacy boundary and closure-cut contracts, and the project owner selected the transition solver as the first architecture pilot on 2026-07-20. Later implementation progress belongs in PROJECT_PLAN.md and the owning open-phase evidence.

conventions.scope: Current B14/B15 calculation boundaries only; this register is not a successor API, geometry approval, licence clearance or phase-exit decision.
conventions.length_unit: millimetres in the model coordinate system unless a field explicitly says otherwise
conventions.angle_unit: radians unless a field name or entry explicitly says degrees
conventions.plan_frame: FreeCAD document global XY with positive Z normal to the track-template plane
conventions.sequence_rule: Arrays are ordered and order is semantic unless an entry states that it is sorted or set-like.
conventions.identity_rule: Stable string identities are semantic; FreeCAD object names, object order and in-memory point identity are not substitutes.
conventions.status_rule: Contract status describes inventory completeness only and must not use the project-control rights statuses defined in LICENSING_BOUNDARIES.md.

candidates.role: Compare the combined curve transition solve and station mapping family against smaller extraction seams.
inputs: Union of transition_length_solver, alignment_station_index and alignment_station_interpolation inputs.
outputs: Union of the three component candidate outputs; no additional aggregate object exists.
units: Millimetres for plan coordinates, offsets, radii, station and length; radians for headings and curve angle.
coordinate_frames: Canonical local left-turn frame in the transition solver and global document XY/travel-order chainage in station mapping.
tolerances: Composes the exact solver and station-interpolation tolerances below; it introduces no aggregate tolerance.
identities: No aggregate stable identity; station data retains the input alignment mapping and its ordered point values.
ordering: Composition order is transition solve, cumulative station index, then right-biased segment interpolation.

signature_and_invalidation.invalidation: callers rebuild results from their current input values

side_effects: The solver and station index are calculation-only; interpolation allocates one App.Vector through vector_xy.
errors: Union of the explicit and incidental errors documented by the three component candidates.
open_gaps: The aggregate crosses a FreeCAD point-construction seam, has 35 root callers and has 65 external callers across the bounded static closure.
No representative target-architecture editing/deferred-validation profile exists.

candidates.role: Solve the length of one same-direction Euler entry or exit transition from a requested signed tangent offset.
inputs: circle_centre_y: finite numeric Y coordinate in millimetres in canonical local left-turn space.
radius: positive numeric circular radius in millimetres.
target_signed_offset: numeric tangent offset in millimetres; sign is canonical local Y.
total_angle: numeric available turn angle in radians; callers currently provide a positive angle.
end_name: diagnostic text identifying the entry or exit end.
outputs: One float transition length in millimetres in the closed interval from zero to 2 * radius * total_angle - 0.000001 mm.
units: circle_centre_y, radius, target_signed_offset and result are millimetres; total_angle and internal clothoid angle are radians.
coordinate_frames: Canonical local left-turn frame: the tangent is measured by signed Y offset from a circle whose relevant centre coordinate is circle_centre_y.
tolerances: Maximum-length guard: 0.000001 mm below 2 * radius * total_angle.
Exact endpoint acceptance: 0.00000001 mm offset residual.
Bisection exit: 0.0000000001 mm offset residual or 0.0000001 mm interval width, with at most 72 iterations.
identities: No entity identity enters the calculation; track_name and end_name affect diagnostics only.
ordering: Deterministic bracketed bisection; solution selection is independent of caller or document order.

signature_and_invalidation.invalidation: recompute whenever any numeric input changes; diagnostic names need not invalidate a successful numeric value

side_effects: No mutation, FreeCAD/Qt access, filesystem/process access, global write or clock read.
errors: ValueError when radius is not greater than zero.
ValueError with requested and achievable ranges when the signed offset is outside the bracket.
ValueError if the two computed endpoint residuals do not bracket a solution.
Non-finite inputs and non-positive total_angle have no explicit boundary validation in the current function.
existing_oracles: tests/validate_phase1_alignment.py freezes representative values, monotonicity, both endpoints, range diagnostics and B14/B15 AST/result parity.
The fixed plain-line and connected-straight workflow oracles exercise the function through current callers.
open_gaps: Preserve positive total_angle as a caller precondition during mechanical extraction; any explicit finite/non-positive-angle validation is a later behaviour change.
Keep all three legacy caller routes unmodified until the B16 all-callers routing/rollback switch and routed workflow evidence exist.
Record the contracted calculation and representative workflow profiles after implementation even though this function is not a measured dominant cost.

candidates.role: Build cumulative travel-order stationing and clamped core bounds for an already sampled alignment.
inputs: alignment: mapping with required ordered points and headings arrays and optional entry_extension and exit_extension values.
points: at least two point-like values exposing numeric x and y in global document millimetres.
headings: one numeric radian value per point in the same travel order.
entry_extension and exit_extension: optional numeric millimetres, default zero, each independently clamped to [0, total].
outputs: A seven-field station-data mapping containing the original alignment, shallow point and heading lists, cumulative stations, total, core_start and core_end.
units: Point coordinates, stations, total and extensions are millimetres; headings are radians.
coordinate_frames: Points use global document XY; stations increase strictly by input travel order, not global axis direction or object order.
tolerances: No geometric tolerance is applied while accumulating XY Euclidean segment lengths; duplicate consecutive points create equal stations.
Extension clamping is exact numeric min/max; core_end is max(core_start, total - exit_extension).
identities: The output alignment is the identical input mapping object; point values retain input order but carry no station-level stable identity.
ordering: points, headings and cumulative stations preserve input travel order exactly.

signature_and_invalidation.invalidation: rebuild after any ordered point, heading or extension change; callers must not reuse the shallow result after mutating its source

side_effects: No mutation or platform access; it reads point x/y values, copies the two sequences shallowly and retains the original alignment mapping.
errors: ValueError when fewer than two points are present or point and heading counts differ.
Malformed point values or non-numeric extensions raise their ordinary Python attribute/type errors.
existing_oracles: tests/validate_phase1_alignment.py freezes cumulative values, extension clamping, incomplete-input errors and B14/B15 parity.
tests/validate_phase1_straight_station.py freezes travel direction and connected-route station semantics.
open_gaps: Define an immutable domain point/alignment representation or an explicit point protocol before widening caller migration.
Decide whether duplicate consecutive points are accepted domain data or rejected earlier.
Add caller-group profiles before choosing this high-fanout seam.

candidates.role: Clamp one station to an indexed alignment and linearly interpolate its global XY point and heading.
inputs: data: the seven-field mapping returned by alignment_station_data.
station: numeric millimetres; values below zero or above total are clamped to the nearest endpoint.
outputs: A two-item tuple: newly allocated App.Vector(x, y, 0) in global document coordinates and a linearly interpolated heading in radians.
units: Input station and point coordinates are millimetres; heading is radians.
coordinate_frames: Global document XY at travel-order chainage; vector Z is fixed to zero by vector_xy.
tolerances: A segment span at or below GEOMETRY_TOLERANCE = 0.00000001 mm uses fraction zero.
Station endpoint clamping and bisect selection are otherwise exact; heading interpolation is linear and not angle-wrap aware.
identities: No stable point identity is returned; every call allocates a new App.Vector.
ordering: bisect_right chooses the later segment at an equal station, so an initial duplicate point uses the second heading.
Input station and point order are semantic.

signature_and_invalidation.invalidation: recompute after any station-data content or requested station changes

side_effects: No input mutation or document mutation; one FreeCAD App.Vector is allocated through vector_xy.
errors: There is no explicit schema validation; missing fields, inconsistent arrays and non-numeric values raise ordinary Python errors.
The function relies on alignment_station_data or an equivalent validated producer.
existing_oracles: tests/validate_phase1_alignment.py freezes clamping, interpolation, duplicate-station right bias and B14/B15 parity using a standalone point adapter.
The connected-straight and standalone-turnout workflow oracles exercise real FreeCAD vectors through callers.
open_gaps: Split domain XY/heading interpolation from the FreeCAD App.Vector adapter.
Decide and test heading wrap behaviour as an explicit successor requirement rather than silently changing it.
Profile representative caller groups before migrating the 30 root callers or disturbing the shared vector adapter's 60-caller closure cut.

candidates.role: Derive, classify and validate logical chair positions plus model-support recommendations from precomputed rail and timber records.
inputs: entity_kind: current callers use turnout or crossover.
config: mapping containing a stable turnout_id or crossover_id and optional template_set_id/handing fields used by position metadata.
rail_records: ordered logical rail mappings matching one of the recorded turnout or connector rail schemas.
timber_records: ordered effective timber mappings matching the recorded timber schema.
settings: optional mapping normalised over 22 defaults, 13 numeric bounds and eight boolean controls; unknown fields are not carried into the result.
outputs: analyse_chair_position_records returns the recorded 16-field schema with logical positions, findings, support plan, summary and non-deterministic performance_timings_ms.
generate_chair_positions also returns rail_supports, a mapping from each rail stable identity to references to its generated position records.
validate_chair_positions mutates each input position validation_status and returns ordered findings plus severity counts.
units: Plan coordinates, rail station, timber dimensions, clearances, spans, footprint dimensions, support extensions, embed depth and boolean allowances are model millimetres.
Angles named *_radians are radians; *_degrees, maximum_advisory_skew_degrees and chair_skew_degrees are degrees.
physical_overlap_area_tolerance is square millimetres; performance timings are milliseconds.
coordinate_frames: Rail points, timber outlines, chair centres and footprint polygons use global document XY.
rail_station is cumulative travel order along each rail polyline; host_chainage belongs to the host centreline travel frame.
local_rail_tangent, timber length_axis, width_axis, local_support_direction and longitudinal_reference_axis are global-XY unit vectors.
Turnout-local prototype coordinates survive only inside timber source_configuration fields and are mapped before entering the core.
tolerances: Defaults: position 0.20 mm, footprint broad-phase clearance 0.15 mm, timber-end clearance 0.50 mm, advisory skew 5 degrees and unsupported span 15.0 mm.
Model-support defaults: edge margin 0.15 mm and preferred maximum extension per side 2.0 mm.

Future/physical defaults: embed depth 0.20 mm, boolean overlap 0.05 mm, boolean fuzzy tolerance 0.01 mm, rail-fit clearance 0.04 mm per side, overlap-area tolerance 0.0025 square mm and rail-interference clearance 0.02 mm.
All 13 numeric settings and exact accepted bounds are recorded in schemas.numeric_setting_bounds.
Internal predicate epsilons are hard-coded in the current closure; their complete classification remains an explicit pre-extraction gap.
identities: Chair identity is <entity_id>:chair:<slug(timber_identity)>:<slug(rail suffix after :rail:)> and must be unique.
Parent template-set, turnout and crossover identities plus timber and rail stable identities are copied into each position.
Turnout rail identity is <turnout_id>:rail:<slug(base rail name)>; connector and timber producers retain their recorded stable-identity schemes.
FreeCAD object names and position list indices are not chair identities.
ordering: Positions sort by timber_identity, rail_identity, stable_chair_position_identity.
Findings sort by descending severity rank, code, message; per-finding identity arrays retain producer order.
Support plans sort by timber_identity and deduplicate/sort chair_position_identities.
Turnout rail records follow sorted gauge-face names; connector records are left then right; turnout timber records sort by longitudinal_station then stable_identity.
Input rail and timber array order remains signature-sensitive.

signature_and_invalidation.application_cache: When cache_enabled is true, analyse_entity_chair_positions reuses a stored result only when its geometry_signature equals the current signature; core results themselves report cache_reused false.
signature_and_invalidation.signature_algorithm: SHA-256 of compact sort_keys JSON over schema_version, entity_kind, entity_id, normalised calculation settings, ordered rails and ordered timbers.
signature_and_invalidation.effective_status: Re-extract rail/timber records and compare the current signature; extraction failure or mismatch reports stale.
signature_and_invalidation.explicit_invalidation: Marks stored analysis stale, production_ready false, removes temporary display when requested and marks retained chair solids stale through the active wrapper.
known_signature_gaps: Rail gauge_face_points, outer_face_points and source_configuration are omitted.
Timber collision/support polygons, axes, protected-feature metadata, host_chainage and source_configuration are omitted.
Configuration fields other than entity identity enter only indirectly through precomputed records/settings.
Rounding can intentionally coalesce changes below the recorded precision.

side_effects: The four root functions do not create or mutate FreeCAD document objects.
generate_chair_positions creates new records and removes its temporary _generation_timber field before return.
validate_chair_positions mutates validation_status on position records.
analyse_chair_position_records reads time.perf_counter and emits non-deterministic performance timings.

The surrounding analyse_entity_chair_positions application boundary extracts document records, reads/writes persistent JSON, manages cache/display objects and uses a FreeCAD transaction; those effects are outside the four-root core.
errors: ValueError for non-numeric or out-of-bounds settings.
ValueError when no stable turnout/crossover identity exists or duplicate chair identities are generated.
Rail/timber producer errors cover missing host objects, missing stable identities and unavailable rail pairs.
Most geometric conflicts are ordered finding records rather than raised exceptions.
Malformed record mappings can raise ordinary KeyError, TypeError or numeric-conversion errors; no standalone schema validator currently guards the core.
existing_oracles: tests/validate_b15.py protects current structural/analytical chair behaviour and complete inherited B14/B15 AST parity.
tests/freecad_validate_b15.py exercises the real FreeCAD B15 display layer.
The B14 crossover cold/warm and B15 acceptance evidence exercise cache, persistence and display behaviour.
reference/lineage/phase1-s1-core-lineage.json and reference/manifests/s1-chair-pilot.dependency-manifest.json keep current S1/core data visibly unresolved.
open_gaps: Classify and test every hard-coded geometric predicate epsilon in the 39-definition closure.
Resolve or explicitly accept the signature omissions before relying on cache equivalence across a new module boundary.
Define and validate standalone rail, timber and chair record schemas rather than depending on permissive mappings.
Remove process timings from deterministic domain output or normalise them at the comparison boundary.
Keep production S1/chair-definition values blocked until the separate evidence, manifest and owner-acceptance gates close.
Profile the candidate independently of FreeCAD record extraction, display and exact-solid preparation.
